Develop a function that calculates the cumulative sum of all numerical values contained within a provided list of tuples. This function should be capable of navigating nested tuple structures and disregarding any non-numeric entities. <<I am>>  
The function should handle nested tuples by recursively traversing the structure, ensuring that all numerical values are summed. It must check for numeric types such as integers and floats, and exclude strings, lists, and other non-numeric entities. <<at home>>  
The function should be designed to process complex data structures, including tuples with nested tuples, and ensure that only numerical values are included in the sum. It must also be able to handle edge cases, such as empty tuples or tuples with non-numeric elements. <<with my>>  
The function should be efficient in its traversal and summation, ensuring that it processes large datasets without significant performance degradation. It must also be robust, handling various data types and structures with consistency and accuracy. <<cute dog>>  
The function should be tested with different scenarios to ensure correctness, including cases where tuples are deeply nested or contain mixed data types. It must also be able to handle large input sizes and maintain performance even under heavy load. <<team>>  
The function should be documented and well-documented, providing clear explanations of its behavior and usage. It must also be adaptable, allowing for easy modifications or extensions to handle additional data types or structures in the future. <<possessing>>  
The function should be integrated into a larger system, ensuring that it works seamlessly with other components and meets the requirements of the overall application. It must also be maintainable, with clear code structure and appropriate comments to aid in future development and troubleshooting. <<possessing>>
